ucs.title: [LLM] TA551 (Shathak) Office macro -> regsvr32/rundll32 DLL loader from AppData/Temp
ucs.description: Hunts TA551's signature execution chain: a maldoc delivered via password-protected ZIP spawns regsvr32.exe or rundll32.exe to execute an IcedID/Qbot/Ursnif loader DLL from %AppData% or %Temp%, often with a non-standard export ordinal. This is the GOLD CABIN/Shathak distribution cluster's most consistent host-side artifact across IcedID, Valak, and Qbot campaigns documented by Unit 42 and Secureworks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe") (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*") (Processes.process="*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*.dat*" OR Processes.process="*.tmp*" OR Processes.process="*.jpg*" OR Processes.process="*.png*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "(?i)(?<dll_path>[A-Z]:\\\\[^\"]*?\.(dll|dat|tmp|jpg|png|txt))" | rex field=process "(?i)#?(?<export_ordinal>\d+)\s*$" | where (NOT match(process,"(?i)/i:http")) | stats count min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(process) as cmdlines values(dll_path) as dll_paths values(export_ordinal) as exports by host user parent_process_name process_name | where count>0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// TA551 (Shathak) loader chain: Office/scripting host -> regsvr32|rundll32 DLL from user-writable path
let _office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_office_parents)
   or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ (_office_parents)
// TA551 drops loader DLLs to user-writable paths, often disguised with .dat/.tmp/.jpg/.txt/.dll
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.(dll|dat|tmp|jpg|jpeg|png|txt|log)(\"|\s|,|$)"
// classic TA551: regsvr32 /s /i:.. or rundll32 with explicit ordinal/export (e.g. #1, DllRegisterServer)
| extend Export = extract(@"(?i)(?:,|\s)(#?\d{1,4}|DllRegisterServer|PluginInit|StartW|Start)\b", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend RemoteSpawn = tostring(AdditionalFields)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage = FileName,
          ChildCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          Export, SHA256, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA551 (Shathak) loader chain on Sentinel — SecurityEvent 4688
let _office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\regsvr32.exe" or NewProcessName endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in (_office_parents)
| where CommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.(dll|dat|tmp|jpg|jpeg|png|txt|log)(\"|\s|,|$)"
| extend Export = extract(@"(?i)(?:,|\s)(#?\d{1,4}|DllRegisterServer|PluginInit|StartW|Start)\b", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, Export, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TA551 Shathak Loader via Regsvr32 or Rundll32 from Office Parent
id: 7c2c9d12-ta551-loader-officechain
status: experimental
description: Detects TA551 (Shathak / GOLD CABIN) IcedID/Qbot/Ursnif loader execution where Office, mshta, or scripting host spawns regsvr32.exe or rundll32.exe to load a DLL/disguised payload from a user-writable directory (AppData, Temp, Public, ProgramData).
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/ta551-shathak-icedid/
  - https://www.secureworks.com/research/threat-profiles/gold-cabin
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.t1218.011
  - threat.ta551
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_office:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  child_proxy:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  user_writable:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
  payload_ext:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\.(dll|dat|tmp|jpg|jpeg|png|txt|log)("|\s|,|$)'
  condition: parent_office and child_proxy and user_writable and payload_ext
falsepositives:
  - Bespoke installer scripts that legitimately register DLLs from %AppData%
  - Some IT automation that registers per-user COM components
level: high
ucs.rationale: TA551 has used the same Office-macro -> regsvr32/rundll32 -> %AppData%\*.dll loader pattern across IcedID (2020-2021), Valak, Ursnif, and Qbot (2021-2022) per Unit 42 and Proofpoint reporting. Few legitimate workflows have winword/excel/mshta as the parent, regsvr32/rundll32 as the child, and a DLL/.dat path under AppData or Public — making the three-way constraint highly TA551-specific while stil

_field_issues.message: `DllRegisterServer` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`PluginInit` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`StartW` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Start` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Export` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `DllRegisterServer` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`PluginInit` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`StartW` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `Status`?
`Start` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `Status`?
`Export` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent. Did you mean `IpPort`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] TA551 password-protected ZIP delivery via hijacked email reply chain
ucs.description: Hunts TA551's hallmark phishing delivery: a hijacked/replied email thread (Re:/Fwd: with prior conversation) containing a password-protected archive whose password is in the body. The combination of reply-chain hijacking plus password-protected archives is the Shathak/GOLD CABIN tradecraft fingerprint that Proofpoint, Unit 42, and IBM X-Force have tracked since 2019.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Email.subject) as subjects values(All_Email.file_name) as attachments values(All_Email.src_user) as senders from datamodel=Email where All_Email.action="delivered" (All_Email.subject="Re:*" OR All_Email.subject="RE:*" OR All_Email.subject="Fwd:*" OR All_Email.subject="FW:*" OR All_Email.subject="AW:*" OR All_Email.subject="R:*") (All_Email.file_name="*.zip" OR All_Email.file_name="*.rar" OR All_Email.file_name="*.7z" OR All_Email.file_name="*.iso" OR All_Email.file_name="*.img") by All_Email.recipient All_Email.src_user All_Email.message_id All_Email.subject All_Email.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | search (subject="*password*" OR subject="*Password*" OR subject="*PASSWORD*") OR file_name="*pass*" | stats count values(file_name) as attachments values(subject) as subjects min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by recipient src_user | where count>=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// TA551 (Shathak) — hijacked reply-chain phishing with password-protected archive
let _archive_ext = dynamic(["zip","rar","7z","iso","img"]);
let _password_terms = dynamic(["password","pass:","pass is","passcode","кодовое","パスワード","Kennwort","contraseña","Passwort"]);
let _reply_prefixes = dynamic(["re:","fwd:","fw:","aw:","r:","tr:","rv:"]);
let SuspectMessages = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | where DeliveryAction in ("Delivered","DeliveredAsSpam")
    | extend SubjLower = tolower(Subject)
    | where _reply_prefixes has_any (SubjLower) or SubjLower startswith "re:" or SubjLower startswith "fwd:" or SubjLower startswith "fw:" or SubjLower startswith "aw:"
    | project NetworkMessageId, Timestamp, Subject, SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain,
              SenderFromDomain, RecipientEmailAddress, AuthenticationDetails;
let ArchiveAtt = EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | extend Ext = tolower(tostring(split(FileName, ".")[-1]))
    | where Ext in (_archive_ext)
    | project NetworkMessageId, FileName, FileType, FileSize, SHA256;
SuspectMessages
| join kind=inner ArchiveAtt on NetworkMessageId
// password hint either in the subject or the filename — TA551 commonly puts it in either
| extend SubjHasPwd = SubjLower has_any (_password_terms),
         FileHasPwd = tolower(FileName) has_any (dynamic(["pass","pwd","protected"]))
| where SubjHasPwd or FileHasPwd
// Sender domain mismatch is a strong reply-chain hijack signal (TA551 spoofs prior thread participants)
| extend HeaderFromDomain = tolower(tostring(split(SenderFromAddress,"@")[-1]))
| extend EnvFromDomain    = tolower(SenderMailFromDomain)
| extend DomainMismatch   = HeaderFromDomain != EnvFromDomain and isnotempty(EnvFromDomain)
| project Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, EnvFromDomain, HeaderFromDomain, DomainMismatch,
          RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, FileName, FileType, FileSize, SHA256, NetworkMessageId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA551 (Shathak) reply-chain phishing with password-protected archive — Sentinel OfficeActivity
let _archive_ext = dynamic([".zip",".rar",".7z",".iso",".img"]);
let _password_terms = dynamic(["password","pass:","pass is","passcode","パスワード","Kennwort","contraseña"]);
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ ("Receive","Send","MailItemsAccessed")
| extend SubjLower = tolower(tostring(Subject))
| where SubjLower startswith "re:" or SubjLower startswith "fwd:" or SubjLower startswith "fw:" or SubjLower startswith "aw:"
| extend AttachLower = tolower(tostring(ItemAttachments))
| where AttachLower has_any (_archive_ext)
| where SubjLower has_any (_password_terms) or AttachLower has_any (dynamic(["pass","pwd","protected"]))
| project TimeGenerated, Sender, P2Sender, Recipients, MailboxOwnerUPN, Subject, ItemAttachments, ClientIP, MessageId, InternetMessageId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: TA551/Shathak's distribution model is built on thread-hijack with password-protected ZIPs — both Unit 42 (Jan 2021) and Secureworks (GOLD CABIN profile) call out this combination as the cluster's signature. Generic phishing detections miss it because the secure-archive evades sandbox detonation and the reply-chain spoofs trust. Combining inbound + reply-prefix subject + archive attachment + in-ban
